title: Task
description: A typed unit of work handed to an agent. The canonical object of handoff between rings. A prompt is a *rendering* of a Task for an LLM; it is not the Task itself.

task_id.description: Content-addressed id: hash(kind, version, inputs, params). Identical Tasks produce identical task_ids — this is what makes caching work.

kind.description: Dotted kind registered in the task catalog (e.g., 'summarize.thread', 'classify.intent'). Each kind defines its own input/output schemas.

version.description: Semver of the kind's schema. Bump major on breaking changes.

inputs.description: Kind-specific input payload. MUST validate against the kind's input schema at the declared version.

params.description: Non-input knobs that influence production but are not semantic inputs (style, length, thresholds, sampling temperature).

idempotency_key.description: Stable key that dedupes retries. Typically equals task_id; may differ when the caller wants to force fresh work despite identical inputs.

budget.description: Hard ceilings the producer must honor. Exceeding any triggers a 'budget.exceeded' error Result, not a silent continuation.

capability_token.description: Scoped authority the producer may exercise. Producers MUST attenuate before delegating further.

provenance.description: Who produced this Task (the caller) and with what context. Downstream agents rely on this to decide trust.
